workspace.note: Use explicit terms for non-canonical meanings (filesystem workspace, session context).

app.note: Differentiate AppTemplate, AppInstance, and Appnode in docs/contracts.

app_transition_mapping.migration_note: Preserve DB/API naming for compatibility; apply canonical names in architecture/specs first.

graph.note: Avoid bare term 'graph' in architecture docs and contracts.

skill.note: Treat as derived/generated layer, not runtime source of truth.

session.note: Public APIs remain session_id; runtime internals may map to sessionKey.

metadata.note: metadata_ remains adapter/ORM alias where required.

roles.note: Frontend roles map to backend role layers, not independent semantics.

context.note: Prompt/prose are views/projections of typed state.

tool.note: Smallest capability unit with explicit input/output/effect semantics.

workflow.note: Ordered/conditional composition of tools and/or subworkflows.

node.note: Use Node for DB hierarchy entity and NodeContainer for runtime typed payload.

container.note: Container semantics are runtime payload semantics, not UI node shell semantics.

identifier_axis.note: Prefer app_id for new cross-layer contracts unless adapter constraints require aliases.

boundary_axis.note: domain is profile label, species is classification taxonomy, api_boundary is enforceable protocol permission model.

runtime_provider_axis.note: Do not collapse provider choice and runtime path into one concept.

agent.note: Agent is executor over state/effects, not persona prose object.

subagent.note: Child executor with bounded capabilities and trace linkage.

appnode.note: UI application surface (e.g., ffs4/ffs5/ffs6), distinct from app template or app instance.
